name: Opsgenie API
what_it_does: Atlassian Opsgenie's REST API for managing alerts, on-call schedules, escalation policies, and incident notifications for DevOps and SRE teams.
use_cases: Creating alerts programmatically from monitoring or CI/CD agents
Querying on-call schedules to determine who is currently on duty
Acknowledging and closing alerts automatically when issues self-resolve
Managing team escalation policies and rotation schedules via automation
Integrating with Jira Service Management for unified incident tracking
not_for: Teams already using PagerDuty who don't need to migrate
Simple notification-only workflows without escalation logic
Non-technical teams without DevOps or SRE context
best_when: An agent needs to trigger or manage alerts within an Atlassian-centric stack (Jira, Confluence, Bitbucket) where unified incident response is needed.
avoid_when: Your team has no existing Atlassian tooling investment, or you need advanced AIOps features beyond alert routing and on-call management.

auth.notes: API keys generated per integration or at the team/account level. Different keys for Alert API vs other endpoints. Keys grant broad access — no fine-grained OAuth scopes available.

pricing.free_tier_limits: Free plan: up to 5 users, basic alerting and on-call
pricing.notes: Bundled with Jira Service Management at higher tiers. Standalone pricing is competitive with PagerDuty. Enterprise tier includes advanced analytics and SSO.

performance.rate_limits: Alert API: 100 requests/minute per API key; other endpoints vary

agent_readiness.error_message_notes: JSON error responses include message and took fields but error codes are less granular than PagerDuty. HTTP status codes are standard. Some edge-case errors are underdocumented.
agent_readiness.idempotency_notes: Alert creation supports alias field as a deduplication key — if an alert with the same alias exists, a new one is not created. Other operations are not idempotent.
known_agent_gotchas: Alert API key is separate from other API keys — using the wrong key returns 401 with no clear explanation
Alert alias for deduplication must be set at creation time; cannot be added retroactively
Opsgenie uses EU and US API endpoints (api.eu.opsgenie.com vs api.opsgenie.com) — using the wrong region returns 401
On-call schedule API returns participants but does not directly indicate who is currently on-call without date math
Rate limits are per integration key, not per account — easy to hit limits when multiple agents share a key
Alert actions (acknowledge, close, snooze) are POST requests to /actions subdirectory, not PATCH on the alert resource

security_score_components.security_notes: API keys are not scoped — a single key grants broad access. No OAuth2 or fine-grained permissions. Keys should be rotated regularly and stored in secrets managers.
